title_en: Rebuilding Syria will cost over $200bn. Bombs are not helping
title_zh: 重建叙利亚将耗资2,000亿美元以上，轰炸并未帮助
rubric_en: Emmanuel Macron is greeted by explosions in Damascus
rubric_zh: 埃马纽埃尔·马克龙在大马士革迎来爆炸声迎接

paragraphs.en: On july 6th Emmanuel Macron, the French president, became the first major Western leader to visit Syria since the toppling of the Assad dictatorship. He brought with him a coterie of French ceos. Mr Macron’s host, Ahmed al-Sharaa, al-Qaeda-militant-turned-statesman, must have been thrilled. He has chased foreign investment and recognition since seizing power 19 months ago. The two bombs that rocked central Damascus the next morning were far less welcome. They are a reminder of the limits of Syria’s recovery.
paragraphs.zh: 7月6日，法国总统埃马纽埃尔·马克龙成为自阿萨德独裁政权倒台以来，第一位访问叙利亚的重要西方领导人。他随行的是一批法国首席执行官。马克龙的主人、前“基地组织”武装成员出身的国会议员兼政治人物阿赫麦德·沙拉必定喜出望外。自19个月前掌权以来，他一直在争取外国投资和国际承认。第二天早晨，市中心大马士革被两枚炸弹震动，远没有那么受欢迎。这再次提醒人们：叙利亚复苏仍然有明显的局限。

paragraphs.en: Nowhere is that clearer than in Jobar, a once-thriving suburb in north-east Damascus. Today it is littered with the concrete shells of looted apartment buildings and the ruins of mosques and shops. There is no mobile-phone signal. Nor is there much evidence of anything being rebuilt.
paragraphs.zh: 在大马士革东北部，这种局限最清晰的体现是乔巴尔区。这个曾经繁荣的郊区，如今到处都是被掠夺公寓楼的混凝土外壳，以及毁坏了的清真寺和商铺废墟。这里连手机信号都没有。更看不到的是，能看出有多少地方正在重建。

paragraphs.en: Since Bashar al-Assad’s regime was overthrown, the much-touted reconstruction of a country devastated by civil war has yet to materialise. A slew of agreements with foreign and domestic firms and a big investor conference in June grabbed headlines. But on the ground frustration is growing. Bureaucracy and old regulations that are “designed for corruption” are choking progress, says a local businessman. Petty graft, political infighting and broken promises are sapping optimism.
paragraphs.zh: 自巴沙尔·阿萨德政权倒台以来，这个被称赞已久的内战废墟重建计划仍未真正落地。六月一系列与外资和本土公司的协议，以及一次大型投资者大会，曾占据了媒体头条。但在一线，失望正在加深。当地一位商人说，繁琐官僚程序和“专为腐败而设”的旧规章正在扼杀进展。小额贿赂、政治内斗以及食言的承诺正在消解乐观情绪。

paragraphs.en: Syria needs to rebuild everything, from houses, roads and bridges to energy and water infrastructure and its patchy telecoms network. One-third of its physical assets have been destroyed. The World Bank puts the total reconstruction costs at $216bn, ten times Syria’s gdp in 2024.
paragraphs.zh: 叙利亚需要重建一切，从住房、道路、桥梁到能源和供水基础设施，再到其支离破碎的电信网络。其三分之一的物理资产已被毁。世界银行估算，重建总成本为2160亿美元，相当于叙利亚2024年国内生产总值的十倍。

paragraphs.en: Syria is painfully strapped for cash. Strict withdrawal limits mean few Syrians are willing to deposit their savings in local banks. Bereft of capital, banks are unable to extend loans. The Syrian pound, a managed currency, has continued to fall against the dollar in recent months.
paragraphs.zh: 叙利亚正因现金短缺而苦不堪言。严格的提款限制意味着很少有叙利亚人愿意把储蓄存入本地银行。在缺乏资本的情况下，银行无法发放贷款。叙利亚镑这一受控货币近几个月持续对美元贬值。

paragraphs.en: Still, if Mr Sharaa has succeeded at anything, it is at diplomacy and drumming up investor interest. His government, he declares, does not want to rebuild Syria “through aid and assistance” but through investment. He has struck up an improbable rapport with Donald Trump, who has eased American sanctions. And he has capitalised on growing interest among Gulf rulers in Syria as a strategic hub, especially after trade through the Strait of Hormuz was choked off.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管如此，如果说沙拉（Sharaa）先生擅长什么，那就是外交和吸引投资者。他宣布，他的政府不想通过“援助和帮助”来重建叙利亚，而是通过投资来重建。他与唐纳德·特朗普建立起了看似不太可能的关系，后者已放宽了美国制裁。他也抓住了海湾国家统治者对叙利亚作为战略枢纽日益增长的兴趣，尤其是在霍尔木兹海峡的贸易被扼杀之后。

paragraphs.en: Gulf investors zoom into Damascus by private jet. Gulf commercial airliners fly in multiple times a day. Qatar has taken the lead: ucc, a construction firm, already has spades in the ground. It is redeveloping an airport that it hopes to open at the end of summer and is ploughing billions of dollars into Syria’s power plants. The Qataris have also acquired a Syrian bank, which will make it easier for them to move money into the country. And they plan to finance a big affordable-housing project in the coming months.
paragraphs.zh: 海湾投资者乘坐私人飞机飞赴大马士革。海湾商业客机每天往返多次。卡塔尔走在前列：施工公司ucc已把铁锹插进土地，开始动工。它正在重建一座希望今年夏末开工的机场，并且在叙利亚电厂投入了数十亿美元。卡塔尔人还收购了一家叙利亚银行，这将使他们更容易将资金转入该国。并且他们计划在未来几个月资助一个大型保障性住房项目。

paragraphs.en: The Emiratis have also shaken off their early wariness of Mr Sharaa. In May Mohamed Alabbar of Emaar, a big property firm in the United Arab Emirates (uae), said he was looking at $18bn-worth of projects in Syria. Emirati delegations have descended on Damascus in recent months to discuss transport, trade and a logistics corridor. Saudi companies and its government have pledged more than $5bn. stc, its biggest telecom firm, is building a 4,500km-long fibre-optic network, data centres and subsea cable-landing stations worth $800m. On June 30th Syria awarded a $750m licence to Zain, a Kuwaiti telecoms operator, which wants to roll out 5g.
paragraphs.zh: 阿联酋的商人也已经打消了对沙拉的早先戒备。5月，阿联酋大型地产公司Emaar的穆罕默德·阿拉巴尔表示，他正在研究价值180亿美元的叙利亚项目。阿联酋的代表团近几个月纷纷赴大马士革，讨论交通、贸易和一条物流走廊。沙特企业与政府已承诺超过50亿美元。其最大电信公司stc正在建设一条长达4,500公里的光纤网络、数据中心和海底电缆登陆站，投资额高达8亿美元。6月30日，叙利亚向科威特电信运营商Zain授予了7.5亿美元的牌照，后者计划推出5G网络。

paragraphs.en: Syrians themselves are also getting in on the action. Hadi Aladdin, a Canadian-Syrian tech entrepreneur, also aims to build a data centre. Samer Chamsi-Pasha, a recently returned exile from a famous merchant family, is lobbying the government to redesignate a disused military airport in Homs for civilian use so he can launch a flight school.
paragraphs.zh: 叙利亚人自己也加入了这场行动。加拿大-叙利亚科技企业家哈迪·阿拉丁（Hadi Aladdin）也计划建设一个数据中心。萨默·查姆西-帕夏（Samer Chamsi-Pasha），这位来自著名商人家族的近来回国的流亡者，正在游说政府将霍姆斯的一处废弃军用机场改造为民用机场，以便他能开办一家飞行学校。

paragraphs.en: Even some American firms are tiptoeing in. Visa and Mastercard have said they are returning. American oil companies will not invest directly but are partnering with regional firms to dip into Syrian oilfields. JPMorgan Chase, an American bank, along with two Gulf lenders, hopes to arrange a $7bn loan, guaranteed by a state-backed Qatari bank, to fund projects led by ucc.
paragraphs.zh: 甚至一些美国公司也在悄然试水。签证公司和万事达卡（Visa 与 Mastercard）已表示将重返。美国石油公司不会直接投资，但正与地区公司合作，逐步进入叙利亚油田。美国银行摩根大通（JPMorgan Chase）与两家海湾地区贷款机构希望筹划一笔70亿美元贷款，由一家由国家支持的卡塔尔银行提供担保，用于资助由ucc主导的项目。

paragraphs.en: For most Western investors, doing business in Syria is still tough. America has, in theory, declared an end to its sanctions there. In reality, a raft of restrictions still prevent big American firms from entering en masse. European and American investors are also confused by the government’s strategy. At a un confab in June, one minister proclaimed a free-market economy, only for another to declare a desire for a “free, planned” one.
paragraphs.zh: 对大多数西方投资者来说，在叙利亚做生意仍然很难。美国在理论上已宣布结束其制裁。然而，在现实中，仍有一大批限制措施使得大型美国公司无法大规模进入。欧洲和美国投资者也被政府的策略弄得困惑。在六月的一次联合国会议上，一位部长宣布实行自由市场经济，紧接着另一位则表达了对“有计划的自由市场”经济的愿望。

paragraphs.en: Syrians, meanwhile, worry that Mr Sharaa has been so busy with diplomacy and investors that he has ignored other problems. Many are disappointed that he has not done more to pursue Mr Assad’s cronies; last month, crowds marched on the homes of officers of the former regime, killing some. Investment conferences and presidential visits are welcome. But one in four Syrians live in extreme poverty. They will judge Mr Sharaa when places such as Jobar are rebuilt. ■
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，叙利亚人担心沙拉先生在外交和吸引投资者方面忙得太多，以至于忽视了其他问题。许多人失望于他没有更进一步追捕阿萨德的心腹。上个月，人群曾游行到前政权官员的住宅，对一些人进行了杀害。投资会议和总统访问当然是受欢迎的，但仍有四分之一的叙利亚人生活在极端贫困中。人们将会在约巴尔等地区重建后评判沙拉先生的表现。■

paragraphs.en: Sign up to the Middle East Dispatch, a weekly newsletter that keeps you in the loop on a fascinating, complex and consequential part of the world.
paragraphs.zh: 订阅《中东快讯》：这是一本每周通讯，让你持续关注这个迷人、复杂且影响重大的世界地区。
